Title: The Southern Book Club's Guide to Slaying Vampires
Author: Grady Hendrix

Title: Legends & Lattes (Legends & Lattes, #1)
Author: Travis Baldree
My Review:
Oh my greatness!! How cute and wholesome was this? Who would have thought that a story of an orc opening a cafe could be so incredibly heartwarming. @travis_baldree created the perfect example of found family in this. The whole book centres around Viv - and Orc - who opens her dream cafe and page by page she attracts creatures to her and the shop and it turns into such a warm and welcoming place for everyone that might be a bit lost themselves. I simply adored Thimble the baking ratkin- and I love how his character grows with each new item he creates. This book has it all, a little romance, loyal friendships, character growth and loads of giggles along the way. I found this very endearing and thought provoking. If we all could just support and encourage someone that is a little off track can you imagine what the world would be like!
If you need a little pick-me-up, this is the book for you!!

Title: The Magickal Summer of Evie Edelman
Author: Harriett de Mesquita

Title: White River Crossing
Author: Ian McGuire
My Review:
This was a hauntingly beautiful tale of survival and dare I say ambition gone wrong. A tale set in the 1700's at a remote outpost of the Hudson Bay Company already brings to mind pictures of struggling to survive in harsh conditions add in the hostility with the indigenous people and you are set for what was truly an amazing story. A troop of 6 set off to go find gold in an even more desolate area than where they live. The trek there comes with many challenges along the way. I loved how each member in the parties characters developed and you really get to know them. Ian McGuire paints a vivid picture not just about the landscape but the people as well. It struck me many times through this novel how difficult live was back then and how thankful we have to be each day to have technology and infrastructure like we do. Ultimately this was a tale that emphasised what greed will do to a person and what some people will do to obtain what they believe is precious.

This book will go down well with any avid historical fiction fan. It left me pondering many things afterwards and even a consideration to go see Hudson Bay.

This Paperback was sent to me by @simonschusteruk and I would like to thank them for this Advanced copy and many thanks to Ian McGuire for such a beautiful tale.

This will be released in February 2026 - keep your eyes peeled for this one - it's truly unforgettable.

Title: The Dying Place (DI Murphy and DS Rossi, #2)
Author: Luca Veste

Title: We Used to Live Here
Author: Marcus Kliewer
My Review: This was so creepy! I was on edge through this whole bloody book! @marcus_kliewer delivered a spine chilling horror/mystery as his debut novel and it was amazing!  Bringing into the book real phenomenon like capgras syndrome and the Mandela effect (there are a few more) add in a creepy isolated house and we are set for what can only be described as a bizarre tale. A family show up on Eve's doorstep and what happens after that is just strange and perplexing. I felt just as unsure and confused as Eve for the latter part of the book and even now after finishing, I am still trying to make sense of it all. Simple things we do everyday like misplace a phone turns into a mystery and honestly this made this entire book so much more real. Fantastic debut, would recommend 100%!

Title: Captured by the Fae Beast (Monsters of Faery, #1)
Author: Mallory Dunlin

Title: Emily Wilde's Encyclopaedia of Faeries (Emily Wilde, #1)
Author: Heather Fawcett

Title: Perfect Remains (D.I. Callanach, #1)
Author: Helen Sarah Fields
My Review: Wow!! This was thrilling!! @helenfields_author combines all things gruesome and thriller together for the perfect start to a new series! Not just is there some seriously demented torture techniques in here but also some first class pshycological mind games. This combined with excellent police procedure, guarenteed that I am now hooked on this series! We meet DI Callanach and start his series debut on a seriously twisted case. I love his character and his back story and I can sense only good things to come! Action-packed from start to finish- ideal for any thriller fans.

Title: The Mirror (The Lost Bride Trilogy, #2)
Author: Nora Roberts
My Review: This was beautiful and sad but also hopeful. I love the lost brides trilogy and am impatiently waiting for @norarobertsauthor to release the last book. This is such a beautiful story. In this book we were given much needed insight into the history of the 7 brides and their companions. I love that we spent more time with Sonya and the rest of the cast, just doing normal things. She has grown so much between the first and second book and she is really becoming a strong independent woman regardless of her past. The tragedy at the manor pulls at all the heart stings and I had to blink away some stray tears on more than one occasion. I love Chloe and her sassy mouth and can not wait to see what waits for our gals in the last book!

Title: Spider Bones (Temperance Brennan, #13)
Author: Kathy Reichs
My Review: It's no secret that I am a devout @kathyreichs fan and this book cemented that fact once again. I find the cases in these books fascinating, the history and the amount of research and detail that goes into them blows my mind every time! In this book we get pulled into the workings of JPAC, a military branch that specialises in the recovery of soldiers remains that perished in war - specifically with this case - Vietnam. After finishing the book I went and read up about this and it is truly fascinating as well as heartbreaking. Some of these soldiers get reunited with their family's 40 or 50 years after they have died which in itself is a huge achievement. Tempe once again gets sucked into a side case which turns out to be gang related and her family becomes targets as well. I love how fast paced and action packed these books are and I love the medical aspect of these investigations. Book 13 in the series and it just keeps getting better!

Title: Phantasma (Wicked Games, #1)
Author: Kaylie Smith
My Review: Oh my greatness!! I absolutely loved this! This was so much fun to read, @kaylsmoon knows exactly how to grab your attention and hold it! The story kept flowing and there was never a dull moment. Moving between Ophelia and Genevieve's life story giving us background,  to the romance with Blackwell which got pretty spicy, and then to the epic game to survive the nine levels of Hell whilst being among devils and ghouls and everything else terrifying! The trials where so well thought out, it felt like I was there, each emotion and though described in detail - it was exquisite! It felt creepy and dark - a delicious gothic romantasy! Highly recommend to all fantasy buffs!

Title: Body Double (Rizzoli & Isles, #4)
Author: Tess Gerritsen
My Review: This 4th book in the Rizzoli and Isles series had me glued to my screen. @tess.gerritsen is truly a master at creating a truly horrific psychological thriller. Not just does Maura discover she has a twin but the details of her adoption and her family history comes into question. This was heartbreaking to read and as the story unfolds she is faced with the old fashioned question of nature vs nurture you can't help feeling drawn to her plight. A fantastic continuation of the series and you start to really get invested in the characters. Absolute must for police procedure fiends.

Title: The Shadow Network (WW2 Secret Agent #2)
Author: Deborah Swift
My Review: With this second book in the series @deborahswiftauthor proofs once again how historical fiction can be complex psychological thrillers as well. This was fast paced and had me on the edge of my seat all the way through. I loved that Neil Callaghan from the first book was prominent in this, it made it feel very familiar and I loved that he got his story as well.  Lilli the FMC is such a strong character and had a lot thrown at her, which to be fair she was amazing at dealing with. What was fascinating was that the book was about Britain's "black propaganda " radio broadcasts that were done to sow dissent in Germany. An excellent historical fiction that should be read by all history buffs!

Title: Kings of Quarantine (Brutal Boys of Everlake Prep, #1)
Author: Caroline Peckham

Title: Babel
Author: R.F. Kuang
My Review: I really struggled with this one. Between the linguistics  lectures ( yes it felt like I was back in school!) and the talk about racism, colonialism and slavery I lost the plot. This was obviously not the book for me. Although this was beautifully written it does not help the fact that I did not connect with this book. Dark academia fans will love this.

Title: Curtains for Ginger: Short Stories for Grown Ups Volume One
Author: Emma Sheehan
My Review: What a lovely collection of short stories! I had a good few giggles and was shocked a few times more! Just the right thing to take your mind of life for a few minutes!

Title: Curtains for Ginger
Author: E.E Sheehan
My Review:
Cute and funny

What a lovely collection of short stories! I had a good few giggles and was shocked a few times more! Just the right thing to take your mind of life for a few minutes!

Title: The Intruder
Author: Freida McFadden
My Review: @fmcfaddenauthor does it again! What a twisted dark and sad thriller this was! I love the back and forth between now and then, you never quite know where exactly it's heading, just that you need to pay attention and there will be some serious twists at the end! I have to say this one was very sad for me, there was a lot of trauma and emotional damage to work through. Loved the end!!! Bittersweet at it's best! Pshycological thriller fans, I know you will all love this one.

Title: Heart-Shaped Box
Author: Joe Hill
My Review: This book was creepy and terrifying from the first page. I mean what did you expect from a book where a guy buys a ghost. @joe_hill certainly have a gift for the twisted and macabre. What the blurb doesn't tell you is that this is also a book about love and dealing with the past. As I uncovered the many layers this tale has and you get down to the horrible truths I could not help being sad for these characters and what they have lived through. The tension in this book is palpable and you literally sit on the edge of your seat the whole way through. Definitely a must read for horror fans!

Title: The Hitchhiker’s Guide to the Galaxy (Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy, #1)
Author: Douglas Adams
My Review: The perfect cure for a miserable rainy Thursday. This book is hilarious and absurd and the most fun I had this week! Travel the universe with Arthur and Ford and escape this world in exchange for a ridiculous tale filled with entertaining banter and obnoxious aliens. You will pick up useful information like always have a towel and keep a babel fish to help with communication. In the end the only thing you need to know about this book is that the answer to it al is 42!

Title: Weyward
Author: Emilia Hart
My Review: A heartbreaking yet empowering tale of the woman of the Weyward family. @emiliahartbooks takes us on a vivid journey through the lives of these woman and their tales of heartbreak and the resilience to get through these times. Each woman and era brings it's own unique challenges. I love how the strength for these woman is build on the knowledge of their predecessor making it through an equal or worse situation and how they rose above it by their deep bond with nature albeit being cast aside as witches. I think this gave a beautiful message to all woman, you are stronger than you think and you can change your situation. A lovely woman's historical fiction perfect for Halloween!

Title: Under Your Scars (Under Your Scars, #1)
Author: Ariel N. Anderson
My Review:
What. The. Actual. Fuck.
This is a Dark Romance Tragedy... yes read that again a tragedy, how do you even recover after this.... @ariel.n.anderson gives trauma a new meaning. There is so much love and destruction, pain and healing, raw emotion, control and dependancy.... a book filled to the brim with nothing but devastation and survival. This has left me reeling and I'm not okay, this was brutal and soul crushing, but in the best kind of way. Triggers, so many triggers please make sure you know what you are doing before starting this. Tonight I'm hugging each one of my loved ones and checking that that they are okay and I bet you will do the same once you've read this.

Title: The Englishman (The Englishman #1)
Author: David Gilman
My Review: What an intense book! David Gilman had my full attention from the first chapter. This was fast-paced packed with action right out the blocks! The captivating writing and plot had me mesmerised. There is a hint of historical fiction in this and the research and scene setting was excellent, we were transposed to a few different locations and I could vividly build these in my mind. But let's not stray from the the fact that this was an excellent espionage thriller. Raglan is an enigmatic character and I want to know more! For any action junkie this book will sate your need.

Title: The Women of Wild Hill
Author: Kirsten Miller
My Review: I love @kirstenmillerbooks books, they have strong characters and always a powerful message and this was no different!  I adored every single one of the witches we met and their back stories. When they have to unite to wage a war on the patriarchy it gave for excellent reading. Each one of the Duncan witches had a quality or trade that I could relate to making them all strong and witty main characters that I could not choose a favourite from. Between their squabbling and rage they managed to pull together and give the 'old one' what she wanted. Powerful females together can do so much and Miller knew exactly how te deliver! Historical fiction meet paranormal with a side of feminism what and incredible tale!

Title: A Discovery of Witches (All Souls Trilogy, #1)
Author: Deborah Harkness
My Review: This was not what I expected when I started reading this, it was so much better! @debharkness made me fall in love with Oxford and the Bodleian and the history that made this novel amazing I loved that Diana denied herself magic and we as readers came along for her discovery journey. I loved that this plays out in the real world it made the fantasy element so realistic and plausible you can't help but imagine yourself in this world looking for vampires and deamons and of course witches! Every element in this was so well thought out I was left speechless a few times! (I mean the house that communicated was just incredible! ) i am now super excited to start book two and see where Diana and Matthew is going next! For the fantasy fans that's looking for a modern day story with creatures and magic this is for you!!

Title: The Last Girl To Die
Author: Helen Sarah Fields
My Review: What starts off as a slow-paced small town murder mystery quickly escalates into a heart pounding unsettling thriller. Sadie is called to a small town to solve the ritual like murder of a young girl. As you systematically learn more about the creepy history of The Isle of Mull, you can't help getting goosebumps and constantly checking that dark corner in your bedroom. @helenfields_author sets the perfect scene for this suspenseful witch hunt. No one is truthful and the community is covering up its sordid past as fast as she can uncover it. There were so many unexpected twists and tragedy in this i could not help feeling spent and empty after finishing this. What an incredible and powerful book! The suspense did not let up and you will be reeling by the end! Just incredible!

Title: Nash Falls (Walter Nash, #1)
Author: David Baldacci
My Review:
Incredible! What a rollercoaster that was! Walter Nash is my new favourite vigilante! @davidbaldacciauthor takes us on a terrifying journey into the underbelly of Sybaritec Investments and how this is connected to Walter Nash. It starts off very innocently for Nash until his family is involved. Our meek and mild MMC then starts his transformation, which was incredibly detailed, into the new and improved vigilante hero. I loved being part of the process, the angst was palpable all the way through and you can't help feeling your heart beat racing as the book continues to build towards a climax. The audio cast of @macleodandrewsactor @yolakin Larry Herron @shiromi.arserio and @thewillcollyer was simply incredible! The production plays out like a movie and each actor did their role to perfection! The only negative for me is the fact that we have to wait for book number two! If you are looking for high stakes thrill and action with a vigilante driven by revenge then this is most definitely for you! This will be released on 11 November 2025 - keep your eyes peeled you don't want to miss this!

Thank you to @netgalley, @hachetteaudio and @grandcentralpub for this advanced copy in exchange for my honest opinion.

Title: Inheritance (The Lost Bride Trilogy, #1)
Author: Nora Roberts
My Review: Oh my goodness! I absolutely loved this! This is my first novel by @norarobertsauthor, and I am a fan! This was incredible! Slow burn romance, a healthy dose of mystery, and of course loads of ghosts!. The story is captivating, and I loved the characters and how they developed throughout this book. And the cliffhanger at the end .... It's just crazy and of course, I'm invested now, so I've already ordered the second one!! Incredible writing and a must-read for all romance, and I almost want to say a little historical fiction readers as well.

Title: A Language of Dragons (A Language of Dragons, #1)
Author: S.F. Williamson
My Review: What an incredible read! This was a delightful combo of fantasy and historical fiction. An incredible tale surrounding languages and code breaking @sfwilliamsonauthor has created the perfect balance between reality and fantasy. I loved Vivian and how she changed throughout the story as she figured out what truly matters. Yes, there is a delicious slow burn love, but there is also so much heartbreak. The Dragons are amazing, my favourite being Chumana, of course - such a sassy in your face pink dragon. If you are looking to escape to a dystopian world with war and rebels and dragons, this is for you!

Title: Chasing the Dead (David Raker, #1)
Author: Tim Weaver
My Review: After reading a few of the Raker series out of order, I've decided to start at the beginning, and what a good decision! Discovering Raker's past has made me love him even more. @timweaverbooks masterfully guides us through this shocking and gruesome mystery with shocking twists you don't expect! This was very well thought out and gruesome enough to keep you on your toes all the way through. I am looking forward to the next book in the series - this is going to be an enjoyable journey!

Title: Silent Scream (Anna Travis, #5)
Author: Lynda La Plante
My Review: The 5th instalment of the Anna Travis series did not disappoint. Another fast paced Crime thriller with Anna doing what she does best! @lyndalaplantecbe used her years of film experience to her advantage in this book, as the team is searching for the killer of a famous actress. In true Travis style, there has to be turmoil in her personal life, and of course, Langton is involved. I love that there is so much police procedure ( the good and the bad!) It makes for very good reading being able to follow the case in this way, you get a much better understanding of what and why something works or not.

Title: Her Buried Past (Detective Madison Knight #14)
Author: Carolyn Arnold
My Review:
I immediately fell in love with Detective Madison Knight! No nonsense, strong-willed, and tenacious to a fault! @authorcarolynarnold gives us a perfect crime novel! Jam packed with action and suspects. The police procedural was on point, and there were so many legit suspects i was constantly changing my pick for the muredere. Terry and Madison are a great duo, and I loved the banter. There is nothing better in life than a badass female crime series, and I think I've found my new fix! This is the 14th book in the Madison Knight series, and it's being published on 16 October 2025. This is the perfect book for any crime lover!

Thank you to @netgalley
and Hibbert & Stiles Publishing for this advanced copy in exchange for my honest opinion.

Title: Masters of Death
Author: Olivie Blake
My Review: I really struggled with this one. Although beautifully written, this was very confusing. There are so many characters that you dont really get to know any of them, and there is so much bickering it just felt like chaos. I loved the blurb, but that was not essentially what the book was about, so I felt lost and not really understanding what I just read.

Title: 3 Days, 9 Months, 27 Years (The Time Traveler's Passport)
Author: John Scalzi

Title: The Daughter
Author: T.M. Logan
My Review: What a roller coaster this was! Something so ordinary as sending your child to university was turned into the most terrifying situation you could possibly imagine. Thanks @tmloganauthor a new fear is unlocked! The angst and desperation from this mother was spot on! The plot was engaging and fast-moving, and the twists were incredible! I love that this is such a plausible situation it makes the fear and the thrill of this book so real, and you can easily imagine this in a real-life scenario. This is one of those books that will play on your mind for a long time after you are done with it.

Title: The Mindf*ck Series (Mindf*ck, #1-5)
Author: S.T. Abby
My Review: What an incredible story! The amount of planning and detail that went into this is mindblowing. As the reader, you get systematically taken through the horros that was committed to this family. Lana is such a strong character, and you can't help falling in love with her and her crusade to uncover the truth. You will be morally compromised after reading this book. Certain things just make sense, and this definitely did! This was brutal and bittersweet. My heart broke every time we were let in another piece of the puzzle. This is now officially one of my all-time favourite series ever. What makes this even sadder is the fact that @cmowensauthor is no longer with us. This book will make you laugh and cry and question your morals a few times over, but it is so worth it all!

Title: Top of the Morning!: Short Stories for Grown Ups Volume Two
Author: Emma Sheehan

Title: The Silk Code (WW2 Secret Agent #1)
Author: Deborah Swift
My Review: This was truly one of the best historical fiction books I have read this year. The amount of research that @deborahswiftauthor put into this book is just incredible. This is a brutal and tragic telling of Englandspiel in WW2. This was a sobering look into the reality of war and the devastating after effects. I loved the inside look into code breaking and how it evolved, and yes, sadly, how it was used by the enemy as well. Nancy was such a great, strong female lead character - I loved her strength and resilience. This was my first book by this author, and I am really looking forward to the next one. This is such a great series for any historical fiction fans - you will not regret reading this!

Title: Little Stranger (The Web of Silence Duet, #1)
Author: Leigh Rivers

Title: The Hotel Avocado (Gary Thorn, #2)
Author: Bob Mortimer
My Review: This was such a fun and quirky read! @realbobmortimer had me giggling the whole way through. The audiobook was fantastic as the cast of narrators made the characters come to life. I haven't read the first book in the series yet, but I will now. I need to know more about Gary! What I loved was the utter chaos in here and the hilarious ways that it gets dealt with. I loved the style of this book, and I am looking forward to more from him.

Title: The Enchanted Greenhouse (Spellshop, #2)
Author: Sarah Beth Durst
My Review: I love this series, and I loved this book! @sarahbethdurst has created yet another cosy magical island for us to visit, and it is epic! On this island, we find another very grumpy yet considering MC along with more talking plants and dragons and, of course, a cat with wings. Reading this is like walking into a fairytale and its soft and squishie and makes you yearn for something you didn't know you lost. @caitlindaviesny as the narrator makes this book irresistible, and I implore you to listen to the audiobook. Her voices and dramatisation shoot this book into another dimension. This is all about found family and forgiving and second chances. If you need a book that leaves you content after finishing it, this is it! I can't wait for the third book in this series! It's truly a magical world !

Title: No Time for Goodbye (No Time For Goodbye, #1)
Author: Linwood Barclay

Title: The Secret of Secrets (Robert Langdon, #6)
Author: Dan    Brown
My Review: Wow, this was intense! I missed Robert Langdon, and I missed how intense @authordanbrown's writing is, his attention to detail and the amount of research he had to do is commendable.Most of this happened in the span of a day, which made it fast-paced with constant action and very intense descriptions and discusses about consciousness and near death experiences. It was fascinating to deep dive into this, and although very complex, it was explained very well. There were a fair number of twists, and when all was revealed, I was astounded. Brilliant read,I would recommend highly recommend this to all thriller fans. It's complex and intense, and you need to grasp the concepts, and when you do, you can't put it down!

Title: Rattle (The Bone Collector, #1)
Author: Fiona Cummins
My Review: This was a gripping psychological thriller right from the start. @fionacumminsauthor created a truly sick killer. The fact that there were children involved added  an extra layer of terror, and the fear and lack of understanding from them was palpable. Reading this literally made my skin crawl! It ends very cunningly on a cliffhanger, and yes, I already have the next book lined up! This is a must-read for all the crime and horror fans.

Title: Wolfsong (Green Creek, #1)
Author: T.J. Klune
My Review: If you had told me three days ago, I would fall in love with a series about gay werewolf shifters, I would have laughed. And yet here I am three days later, and TJ Klune sucked me into the Green Creek series. I want to know everything about the Bennet pack, Gordo and Rico, and all the other crazies. I loved this! The writing style was unique, and it made you embrace how awkward Ox is. You will have to trust me and read it to fully understand what I say. It's a beautiful story about found family and a human and his pack. As much angst and uncertainty as there is here, there is also so much love and understanding about pack pack pack. This is one of those times where words fail me, and all I can say is this was everything.  I need a day or two to recover, and then I will read the next one. Do yourself a favour and get this book. It's one you didn't know you needed, but you do, trust me, you do.

Title: The Twat Files: a Life of Mistakes - No Regrets
Author: Dawn French
My Review: A delightful collection of her most embarrassing moments!

Title: The Secret Life of an Uncool Mom (Tara Gallagher, #1)
Author: Serena Terry
My Review: This was hilarious! I love @serenaterry, and I loved that she narrated this herself! I was in stitches the whole time. This is an unfiltered look into the background of a mom trying to deal with a work-life, a husband, and raising 3 children. This was so unapologetically raw and truthful. Dealing with a teenager has never been more funny, and it makes me feel ok with the fact that most days, I think I am failing at raising my own teenager. I love that Tara and Paul are portrayed as happily married, and it adds to drama between them and Gemma. This is a call to all mom's and even dad's to get this read it have a good laugh and remind your self that your little poppet is still alive despite the fact that you embarras them to death! There is a second book in the series, and you can bet your ass I'm reading this next!

Title: Young Love
Author: Suzanne Ewart
My Review: This was such a puzzling story! @suzanneewart_author managed to convey every single bit of the angst and confusion between the two main characters, Kelsey and Lewis. I was constantly switching between confusion, anger, and pity for both. There is so much happening in their lives - it all makes sense, but also I want to shake them and say just spill the tea man!! The ending was bittersweet and a good end to both stories!

Title: Why Mommy Swears
Author: Gill Sims
My Review: I always have such a good laugh with @judgydogandgill books! She is truly gifted with how she manages to capture the catsuit crazy things we have to endure and do as parents! This was so much fun to read. Ellen is one of the best fictional characters i have come across this year! The story is chaos and hilarious with a good dose of reality. You can't help but want to know more about mummy's precious poppets and the utter chaos that surround this family. I highly, highly recommend this to all readers!! It's the most fun I had sitting down in a long time!

Title: The Curator (Washington Poe, #3)
Author: M.W. Craven
My Review: If you know me and have spoken to me , I would have recommended the Poe and Tilly series to you multiple times! I love how @m.w.craven carefully plotted out their very different personalities and managed to create a dou that complement each other in the most obscure way! Poe can see connections that most can't and his out of the box way of thinking often solves the case, while Tilly is blunt to the point of being funny and always manages to reel Poe back to reality while cleverly supporting his instincts all the while Poe is fiercely protecting her from anyone who cares question her sayings and methods.  She is fierce and loyal, and I love her interactions with just about everyone. This case was intricate and complex with a killer who uses a sickening way of using innocent people to commit his crimes. This was fast-paced and complex, and the last 100 pages had my heart in my throat, and I could not believe who the mastermind was in the end. Excellent police work and an intriguing storyline. And yes, I would recommend this a million times over.

Title: Without Fail (Jack Reacher #6)
Author: Lee Child
My Review: @thereal_leechild has created such a powerful character in Reacher, I dont think i will ever tire of his stories. Reacher is tasked with "assasinating" the vice president. He is employed by his brothers ex (which adds just a tad of drama to the book as well, true Reacher still - we didnt expect anything less), and he needs to try to find holes in the vice-president's security detail. Reading this two weeks after Charlie Kirk was publicly assassinated and the focus from news channels on this senseless murder just gave me goosebumps and made this book hit home more than I think it would on another occasion. As we delve deeper into why the assassin's are after the vice president - there comes a sad reality that these people will come up with any excuse to execute their plans as trivial as the reason might be. This was a powerful and sad book, and Reacher, as always, is right in the middle of all of it! Fantastic read for any crime fans.

Title: Find You First
Author: Linwood Barclay
My Review: What a thrill ride this was! The book starts off slow - there is a lot of information to process and viewpoints from different people. But once you fully understand what is happening, @linwoodbarclay ramps up the action, and I went flying through the next 300 pages! I loved the thrill of the chase with this book. It was nerve-wracking and kept me geussing the whole time. What made this so interesting was  the why...(can't say much!) I loved Miles and Chloe, and I thought the ending was bittersweet. A good psychological thriller needs to keep you on your toes the whole time, and this did just that!! This was an excellent book club pick for Tiffany's Tales Book Club - there are so many morally grey areas that we are going to discuss!!

Title: Seven Reasons to Murder Your Dinner Guests
Author: K.J. Whittle
My Review: I absolutely loved this book! Right from the start, you get the impression that the guests attending this special dinner are not the best examples of humans, and suddenly, the title makes sense. It was a colourful story with each of them so vastly different than the next and also how each one approached their death number. I loved Jacqueline King's narration. Her voice added an air of poshness to the book that made their situations seem very final and matter of fact. @kerryjourno took a popular murder theme and added her own twist, and it was suberp! In the end, we can hardly explain why people kill, but her take on it was hilarious and absurd. I did think Vivian was the best character out of all of them and felt good about how it ended. Great debut novel, I really am excited to see what she does next! Whodunit fans, this is the perfect cosy Murder mystery for a cold autumn day under the covers.

Title: The Only Woman in the Room
Author: Marie Benedict

Title: Daisy Jones & The Six
Author: Taylor Jenkins Reid
My Review: This was such an interesting Audiobook! A full cast and in the format of an interview rather than a story. I loved how it jumped perspective, giving insight into a situation from multiple view points, this also cemented that people experience things so differently and no two stories are usually the same as it is the viewpoint of each individual. I thought it was actually very sad and shows that you can't judge a person's happiness unless you walk in their shoes. @tjenkinsreid is a master at creating believable stories. With all her books, I've wanted to do research and learn more about the subject only to realise that it doesn't exist. She transposed you into a different reality, and as such, I think that's the highest praise you can give an author.

Title: Mockingjay (The Hunger Games, #3)
Author: Suzanne Collins
My Review: Bittersweet comes to mind after finishing this book. @suzannecollinswritesbooks gave us the best ending but with so much loss.  The pressure to lead a rebellion has a huge impact on Katniss, and she is still only a girl. All the turmoil and emotions are palpable, and you can feel her spiralling out of control with each new challenge. With each question and answer of real or not real, my heart broke just a little bit more for what they all had to endure. Brilliant use of characters to let the readers know that anything can change in the blink of an eye and anyone can die.

Title: Threat Level: Obsession: A.S.S. Series Book 1 (Axiom Security Specialists)
Author: Anna Heck

Title: Monsters Walk Among US (Ruthless Monsters Book 1)
Author: Gracie   Williams
My Review:
Oh my greatness, @graciewilliamsauthor just wrote one of the best Dark Romance books I have ever read! This was simply superb! Silas intrigued me right from the start, I love his code that he follows, and I love how committed he is to rid the world of evil. What makes a great monster, a man with morals. The imagination that goes into the kills is simply exquisite, and the vengeance is so sweet. Char makes for an amazing broken little side kick, and I enjoyed how her story connects with his. This was brutal and spicy, the perfect mix of good and evil. The only thing I'm sad about is that i have to wait for the next book!

Now for the great news!!! It's released today!!! Dark romance readers, you do not want to miss out on this!


Thank you to Gracie Williams for this advanced copy in exchange for my opinion.

Title: Deadly Intent (Anna Travis, #4)
Author: Lynda La Plante
My Review: I think this was my favourite in the Anna Travis series so far! @lyndalaplantewriter sure knows how to keep you on your toes and geussing! This was such a good and intense case with so many little twists! I loved how the answer always seemed to be just out of reach, and the puzzles pieces not falling into place. Excellent detective work as always from Anna. I loved how there is a new love interest for Anna (maybe two), and it was good to see the return of Langdon to shake things up! Very good series to follow for any crime buff!

Title: Hide and Seek (Helen Grace, #6)
Author: M.J. Arlidge
My Review: I love DI Helen Grace. She is such a strong, independent character and has overcome so much to get where she is. When she was locked up at the end of the last book, I knew this was going to be good. And boy did @m_j_arlidge deliver! This was fast-paced, action-packed, and terrifying. How do you catch a serial killer in prison while in prison... well, read the book and find out! The honorary mention goes to Charlie for her tenacity. We can all do with a friend like her. I am so excited to see what Grace tackles next!

Title: Atmosphere
Author: Taylor Jenkins Reid
My Review: What a book! @tjenkinsreid had me from the very first chapter, sobbing like it was my best friends up there. This was so well written and researched. It felt so real and authentic that you have to stop and tell yourself this is not real and these people don't exist. Numerous times, I wanted to google stuff, realising I can't. The emotion in this was palpable throughout. I can't even begin to imagine what it was like back then if you were different, and that made it even more sad. "Just the act of falling in love was to agree to a broken heart." I don't even know who I would suggest this to, I guess everyone!  Everyone should read this!

Title: The Undomestic Goddess
Author: Sophie Kinsella
My Review: I absolutely loved this! This was hilarious and witty and very relatable! @sophiekinsellawriter is a master at sweeping you away from the dull and dreary and putting you in a crazy scenario to lift the spirits! Samantha is my spirit animal, I mean, am I the only person in this world that has thought I'm going to quit my stressful job and move to the country and try farming.... it was slightly different in the book, but you get what I mean. This is what you want from a book, to wisk you away into a world you can't have but wish you did and make you laugh and forget about your troubles.

Title: 23rd Midnight (Women’s Murder Club, #23)
Author: James  Patterson
My Review: @jamespattersonbooks was the first author I recommended to people, and when I stumbled onto the Woman's Murder Club Series with @maxinepaetro, I was sold on the series right away! Fast forward to now and the 23rd book in the series, and it still gets better after every book! All four of the Murder club gals usually have their own little story in the story, but this time, it is closer to home than what they would want. This was fast-paced and stressful as hell. Every second of listening to this was incredible and packed with so much tension. Highly recommend this series if you have not yet started it.

Title: Desperate Shadows: The Order of the Emerald Mantis Book 1
Author: Helen G. Huntley
My Review:
This was such a refreshing fantasy book. Set in the 1800s, you are transposed to a different world, and the setting and even the speech copies this era. @helenghuntley1 gave such a unique storytelling that I could imagine the smell of the candles and the creaking of the wood benches and buildings - it was a delightful journey for sure. The magic is old and reminded me of the likes of Merlin and such. It is a very good story, and there is always something happening keeping you on your toes the whole way through. In the end, a tiny cliffhanger entices you to read the next book. It's definitely a must-read for all the old school fantasy lovers.

Thank you to @booksirens for my advanced copy in exchange for my honest opinion.

Title: Trust Me
Author: T.M. Logan
My Review: I love @tmloganauthor he always has me on the edge of my seat throughout the book! Ellen is such a strong character, and I loved her tenacity to get to the truth despite the horrors she was put through herself. As a mother, this book was close to my heart, and the fear experienced through this whole book was palpable. Fast-paced, packed full of drama, and the twists just keep coming. Another excellent psychological thriller - I definitely recommend this to the thriller fans.

Title: Profile K
Author: Helen Sarah Fields
My Review: Incredible! This is my first book by @helenfields_author, and did she just blew my mind! What a terrifying, gruesome but amazing story this was! I loved it. Midnight was such an incredible character. Her persistence to figure out what was going on even though she was scared was incredible! The tension was palpable throughout, and I was glued to this book from the start to the finish. It's an amazing psychological thriller, and I can't wait to read more of her books.

Title: Why Mommy Drinks
Author: Gill Sims
My Review: I love @judgydogandgill !! Her books are hilarious and relatable and perfect for a pick me up! This was no different. This is the first in a series of why mommy drinks, and yes, I will be going along for this crazy ride all the way to the end. This is in the form of a diary, depicting the daily life of a midlife aged,  middle-class mom that is barely holding it together. I love the comedy and snark and the relatable way she acts and says things, even to her 'darling angels'. I want to be her friend!! 100% recommend to everyone that just needs a break and a good laugh!

Title: Twisted Friction
Author: Layla Moon
My Review: Holy hell! What did I just read.... @laylamoonwrites just blew my mind! This was so spicy it was surely written in hell. There are so many triggers, so be warned, be aware, and make damn sure you can handle it. This was relentless and non-stop from the very first page. I have never been left speechless,  appalled, and interested at the same time. A dark taboo romance does not cover what went down here, and yet I could not stop. There are some cars, bikes, and racing as well, so not just killing and spice. Read only if you dare. You have been warned!

Title: Echo Burning (Jack Reacher, #5)
Author: Lee Child
My Review: I am such a massive Reacher fan! These books are just so easy and entertaining. Reacher is such a ladies man, and it's no surprise that there are a few damsels in distress that he is helping in this book. As always, pace is fast and action-packed. I love how problem solving is done in such a nonchalant way. He makes every situation and solution seem so obvious and easy. Every time I thought, I figured out what was going on, Lee Child pulled the rug out from under me and raised the stakes again. Brilliant book will 100% recommend and excited to see what book 6 holds!

Title: Dazza's Short Stories: Volume One
Author: Dazza Smith
My Review:
Rating: ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️.5

This eclectic selection of short stories was very entertaining. Having spoken to the author @dazzatheunknownauthor, I gained insight on some of the stories and what they were based on, and I have to say he has a brilliant mind and incredible ideas! There is a story here for each of us, from motorbikes to rap battles and dragons and underwater worlds. The selection was fantastic! My only issue is I want more!!! I want a full story from Dazza, and he certainly has the ability! The stories are gripping and fast-paced. I am looking forward to what else he can give us. If you need a little break from life and looking for something short and sweet, then this is for you!

Title: Tears of the Silenced: A True Crime and an American Tragedy; Severe Child Abuse and Leaving the Amish
Author: Misty Griffin

Title: The Undercutting of Rosie and Adam (Hart and Mercy, #3)
Author: Megan Bannen

Title: The Undermining of Twyla and Frank (Hart and Mercy, #2)
Author: Megan Bannen

Title: The Undertaking of Hart and Mercy (Hart and Mercy, #1)
Author: Megan Bannen
My Review: This was the cutest romantasy I have ever read in my life! Which is an enormous feat if you consider it's a book about disgusting, decomposing bodies and the undertaking of said bodies. @meganbannen turned something gruesome and sad into soft and happy. Hart and Mercy are amazing, and as you progress in the story with the letters and the fact that each of them is pouring their heart out to their friend you slowly start to turn into a fluff ball filled with heart clouds and sunshine. I have never had such a combo in a book! Incredible! From dreary and disgusting to cosy and filled with love. The world building was incredible, and there were so many details put into describing everything. The picture in my mind is so vivid, and even the delicious food from Zeddie left me yearning to taste it. This is not your normal romantasy ... it's better, and I would highly recommend this to every fantasy reader out there.

Title: The Saturday Night Sauvignon Sisterhood
Author: Gill Sims
My Review: @judgydogandgill is my new favourite comedy queen! I absolutely love these so much. The story is relatable and so honest and genuine. The best part is that I could relate to so many things in this book, and it makes me feel better about my life situations. I laughed, I cried, I got frustrated and angry, and it was so exhilarating - a proper roller coaster ride filled with emotion and sassy sayings and making new friends as well as forgiving old ones. Every woman should read these - it's such a relevation!

Title: Why Mummy Drinks on Holiday
Author: Gill Sims
My Review: I have been in a bit of a reading slump, and I needed something to get me out of my book black hole. And let me tell you, @judgydogandgill is a life saver! This book had me from the word go. I was giggling all the way through. And yes, it made me feel very good about myself! To read about other people having to deal with the same boring everyday things I deal with and getting to laugh about.... Brilliant!! I applaud this author, and although this is the 6th book in the series , I am going to get the rest, and, in fact, I am already busy with one of her other books. This was exactly what I needed to get over my depression! If you need a good chaotic laugh, this is the one!

Title: Witchcraft for Wayward Girls
Author: Grady Hendrix
My Review: This book rocked me to my core. A halfway house for unwanted pregnant girls in the 1970s. You can feel the confusion in the girls. You can imagine the fear they feel as they are rejected by society. They dont even keep their names! Thus was such a sobering look into what happens in those days. I was even more surprised by the fact that @riley.sager is a man. He captured their essence so well it was absolutely heartbreaking. And as in any cult classic book, they turn to witchcraft, and it is glorius, frightening, and scary all at once. I loved this so much, and I must say what they did was short of amazing. The audiobook was amazing, filled with so much passion from the  three narrators. You will not be sorry for listening to this!

Title: Middle of the Night
Author: Riley Sager
My Review: This book gave me crazy paranormal vibes right from the word go. The circumstances in which Billy goes missing leave a lot up to the imagination, especially with the weird institution up the road and the stories about what happens there. Nothing seems as it is, and as you slowly start to uncover the events, it becomes clear that foul play is involved. I loved that @riley.sager used a back and forth POV, so you basically knew what is happening in real and past time as it gets solved.

Title: Would You Ask My Husband That
Author: Kathleen Whyman
My Review: It's been a while since I've had a book that frustrated me like this and at the same time made me laugh like I did. @kathleenwhymanauthor took the age-old fight of man against woman to the next level... this time, they are married to each other and competing for the same roles. More than a few times, I wanted to strangle Will for his condescending manner, and at the same time, I  wanted to shake Sarah till she caught a wake-up. A good book should leave you pondering afterwards, and damn did I ponder. I loved this! Touching onto a relatable subject and highlighting the two POV's. This was so cleverly done, and I want to read more by this author! If you are frustrated by life , need a pick up or just a good girly book - this is for you.

Title: The Alchemy of Flowers
Author: Laura Resau
My Review:
This book hit me with all the feels! I did not expect the end at all. @lauraresau writing is so vivid it was easy to get lost in the magic with Eloise. The flowers, the food, the enchantment of the whole castle, and the mystery surrounding it all is such a magical place to get lost in. Eloise could deal with her loss and she could find herself again and it was a magical journey with her to heal and to forgive herself and move on and I was honoured to have been there reading about this journey. Found family has never been more real than in this novel, and this is the true magic in the end if you think about it. Beautiful narrated by @cassandracampbell, her elegant French accent added in a different layer to the already complex and magical story. Perfect read for someone looking for a little bit of self-healing.

Thank you to @netgalley and @harpermusebooks for this advanced copy in exchange for my honest opinion.

Title: Black Summer (Washington Poe, #2)
Author: M.W. Craven
My Review: @m.w.craven is a genius, and I will never stop singing his praises! His understanding of police procedures is phenomenal, and creating a character like  Poe, who is a highly intuitive detective, combined with his unconventional thinking, makes for a powerful, addictive fast paced read with Poe running circles around the other officers and suspects. And it doesn't stop there. Craven gave us another super human in the form of Tilly. This girl is crazy clever. She has some of the best tech skills I've read of, and her lack of social skills paired with Poe gives the reader a non-stop action-packed and complex read that is so addictive. How this case gets unravelled and as things come to light, you have to stop and appreciate the genius that goes into this. It's incredible. I can not recommend this enough! Get ready for a crazy almost unsolvable mystery that will have your heart pounding and your mind reeling.

Title: Eleanor Oliphant Is Completely Fine
Author: Gail Honeyman
My Review: Wow! This book had me experience all the emotions!! Incredible! I loved Eleanor she is wholesome and relatable, and I loved her mannerisms and her sayings. She is who she is and does not care. And then she made a friend and everything changed. I loved going through all of this with her as she experienced a world that did not exist for her. I was frustrated and confused with her, and I felt anger and rejection as she experienced it. The depression was palpable, and her rise from there was amazing. @gailhoneyman shared with us a transformation, and it was magical. These characters will live long in my mind and imagination, and I really hope she will give us another book. This is such a feel good novel- just fantastic - you will not regret reading it!

Title: The Night They Stole My Baby
Author: Darren O'Sullivan

Title: The Serial Killer Support Group (Secret Vigilante, #1)
Author: D.B.   Stephens
My Review: This book had me from the first chapter. My heart was pounding along with the victim, the fear and angst palpable in what I was reading. An issue that is known throughout the world was brought to attention in a subtle yet powerful way. I loved that we had a 3-way POV with the victim, the cop and the killer. For me, the killing was not the main focus but how the victims dealt and processed with their trauma and essentially how they could become strong and move on. This is such an empowering book, and I think @darrensully got the message across in a stunning way! I am now -  team secret vigilante, and I am waiting with bated breath for Book number two.

Title: Accomplice to the Villain (Assistant to the Villain, #3)
Author: Hannah Nicole Maehrer
My Review: This series is incredible, and I adore all of the characters. It is fast-paced and witty, and I had more than a few crying while laughing moments. Evie is the sweetest Lillian on earth, and the banter between Trystan and her is electrifying. @hannahnicolemae must be the queen of slow burn, and it is soul destroying, but in a good way, lol. I love the frustration from both sides, and I want to climb in the story and shake them both. Kingsley still stole the show just because who wouldn't want a frog that could write. This is the most fun you can have while reading a book, and I highly recommend this whole series.

Title: Danger Close: A Second Chance Romance (Mourningkill Book 3)
Author: Molly Briar
My Review:
It's just incredible! I absolutely loved this book, and I just have to read the Morningkill series now - because I want to know everything!!! @molly.briar has masterfully combined crime/thriller with dark and spicy romance! It's a combination that I am going crazy for! Cobra is such a suave character, gentle and caring, but at the same time brutal and unforgiving when it gets to his women. I love that this is a second chance love story, and Cobra and Teri are older and more mature. The fear and angst in Teri felt palpable, and it made my hate towards Ray so much more intense! Dark romance readers, if you are looking for a morally grey MMC and a broken yet strong FMC, then this comes out on the 18th of August!! I guarantee you will want to read the whole series too!

Thank you to Molly Briar for my advanced copy of this delightful book in exchange for my honest opinion.

Title: Luminiferos: The Omen of Light
Author: Yana Metro
My Review: This was absolutely incredible!! @yanametro_author is an absolute master at world building. It was so vivid and beautiful, like I was there myself. The characters are intriquid and each very unique. I loved the magic. It's unusual and complex. The story just flows and is easy on the mind and imagination. I am so excited to see what the rest of the series brings! Lily is an amazing FMC, and her growth in this book was commendable. And can I just add in that Hairito completely steals the show! I seriously feel done in by life for not having my own gargoyle! Fantasy readers, this is incredible. I would triple recommend.

Title: Suck Mommy’s Piss Flaps: An Erotic Killer Story… Or Something (What The F Did I Just Read Collection)
Author: Layla Moon

Title: Glorious Ruins
Author: Judithe Little
My Review:
Initially, I had reservations about this book. It's not like your typical historical fiction- its more a biography of sorts, and once I wrapped my mind around this, and I did some investigating into the main characters, I finally got it. @judithelittle masterfully gives us a glimpse into the lives of these 3 remarkable women. We always put the famous people on pedestals and gush over how lucky they are. What she did was give us the inside look into lives that were actually quite tragic and marred with loss and grief. I felt such intense pity for them after I was done with this and I can't help to think that as famous as they were and always having people around them in the end they were actually very lonely and ultimately just wanted to belong somewhere. I absolutely loved Elisabeth Laglee as the narrator. With her sultry French accented voice, she made the characters come alive, and you would not have gotten that from reading the book. It's a fantastic book that would be amazing for any historical fiction fan.

It will be available on 12 August 2025!

Thank you to @netgalley and @brilliancepublishing for my advanced copy in exchange for my honest opinion.

Title: Human Remains (Kat and Lock, #3)
Author: Jo  Callaghan
My Review: I love this series so much! It feels like I know Kat and Lock like I know my own child. What amazes me is that this is only the 3rd book @jocallaghankat has written - this is an incredible feat! I have read some things from authors who have been around for years, and let me tell you, she has a bright future as an author ahead of her. Lock is learning more about the human condition, and it's endearing but also pretty scary, AI can very likely take over a lot of human jobs in the future and it's something that is on everyone's minds. As per usual police procedure was excellent. The story was fast-paced and left me stunned a few times. And to top it, there were more than a few heartbreaking moments as well as incredible twists!!!  I am super excited to see more of this author, and I am desperately waiting for the 4th book!

Title: The Toy Car: A Short Story
Author: Rose Tremain

Title: Iron Rose (Iron and Steel, #1)
Author: Molly Briar
My Review: This book was incredible!! @molly.briar had me in the first chapter! Rose was amazing in this, so many changes for her in this book, and she just comes out the other end stronger. She basically starts a mob war by not backing down. Alastair is a broody, sexy as hell, possessive mobster who falls head over heals for her. Brett becomes found family, and his only aim is to make her better. There were so many strong characters in this book, and I loved every one of them. And the spice is dark and filthy and oh so good!! Looking for a dark mafia romance, look no more!!

Title: Death and Other Occupational Hazards
Author: Veronika Dapunt
My Review:
Oh my greatness, this was the funniest and cosiest murder mystery I've read all year! @vmdapunt did an amazing job writing this. Death takes a vacation/sabbatical as a person on earth, and as you can imagine, it is hilarious! I love how she tries to fit in and having to navigate emotions and friends and even a love interest. All this while figuring out who is killing people unlawfully.
If you need a pick me up filled with good vibes and laughter, then this is for you!

Title: The Spellshop (Spellshop, #1)
Author: Sarah Beth Durst
My Review: This was the cutest, cosiest romance I have ever read!! This book is going to be what comes to mind if I think of small town romance. @sarahbethdurst must be magical because this was epic! Caz and Meep (you will have to read the book to find out!) stole the show handsdown. Kiela is my new favourite magical gal. She loves books, has Caz and Meep, a hot guy that will build her bookshelves, and so many more fantastic magical creatures. Can i just say I want to be her!! @caitlindaviesny was such a good choice for the narration - she did a phenomenal job, and if you can listen to the audiobook, you will not be disappointed! Give it all the stars - this will stay with you for a while!

Title: We'll Prescribe You a Cat (We'll Prescribe You a Cat, #1)
Author: Syou Ishida

Title: The Housemaid's Secret (The Housemaid, #2)
Author: Freida McFadden
My Review: I love @fmcfaddenauthor books so much! She is the queen of crazy plot twists and phenomenal super tense psychological thrillers that keep you on the edge of your seat. I loved Millie I  The Housmaid, and I love her in this! She is such a badass, so strong, and always willing to help no matter what. This story gets turned around and spat back out in the craziest way, and poor Millie is the one holding the bag. I devoured this in a day, and I can't wait to pick up book 3!

Title: The Dancer and The Masks (Their Obsession, #1)
Author: Bea Paige

Title: His & Hers
Author: Alice Feeney
My Review: What the actual hell!! This book is crazy! Every single time, I thought ok I know who did it, and it makes sense .... then BAM @alicewriterland pulls the rug out from under me and leaves me spinning again. I thought it was everyone. There were legitimate reasons for it to be almost every person in this book, and in the end, I was left stunned again!!! What a fantastic psychological thriller! She kept me thinking and questioning the whole time, and the tension throughout this book was incredible! I read this in a few hours, and I could not put it down. I will definitely be looking for her other books

Title: 206 Bones (Temperance Brennan, #12)
Author: Kathy Reichs
My Review: I love @kathyreichs books! Tempe feels like she is part of my family! She is meticulous in everything that she does, so when she makes a few mistakes in this book, I was already suspicious of where this will go. What I did not see is who and why! Excellent storytelling as always. I love the technicality of these novels and the authors' notes at the back, giving insight into the plot. Another action-packed edge of your seat book that you can't put down!

Title: No Simple Death
Author: Valerie Keogh
My Review: I really should have liked this and I'm really disappointed that I didn't. It's a very good story - the police work was great, and it has gripping suspense, keeping you guessing throughout. It even has a little romance , but I just could not get into it. I didn't like the narrator, so that didn’t help either.  I want to try the next book in the series, but maybe I should get the paperback.

Title: Missing Pieces
Author: Tim Weaver
My Review: Oh my greatness! What did I just read!! This book is crazy! There are so many twists in here it literally left me reeling. First of the characters are so complex, and I felt like I knew them for years, and the fear and angst I felt with Rebekha was tangible. Her will to survive added to the puzzling reason for being stranded in the first place. Slowly, as you start figuring out why the pov from Frank is there, the deceipt and lies start adding up. This is my first novel from @timweaverbooks and will not be my last. I feel he captured the building mystery and tension perfectly, and I will be back for more!

Title: The Sinner (Rizzoli & Isles, #3)
Author: Tess Gerritsen
My Review: I love Rizzoli and Isles, I saw the TV series before I read the books, and this book here is proof that the books are always better! @tess.gerritsen has such an amazing way of bringing the medical facts together with the investigation process. We get to know Maura better in this book, and it's so much better and more detailed than the show! Even though the  focus is on Maura and how her insight is what solves the case, Jane, by no means, is taking a back seat! Their dynamic is incredible, and its only the 3rd book in the series. The mysterious murder of nuns takes us down a deep, spiralling whole where appalling acts get uncovered in the process. Excellent writing and excellent story, I'm invested in this journey!

Title: The It Girl
Author: Ruth Ware
My Review: I seem to be puled towards dark academia these days, and @ruthwarewriter delivered another excellent one for my trophy shelves! This book was filled to the brim with snobs, sex and scandals! I loved every deranged minute of it! As you all know, I love morally grey characters, and there were so many of those in here! Excellent storytelling keeps me guessing till the very end, and it was epic! There are so many secrets and relevations in this book that you will never have a dull moment! If you are looking for a twisted college/university whodunit, then this is for you!!

Title: Part of Your World (Part of Your World, #1)
Author: Abby Jimenez
My Review: If all romance books were written like this, I would be a romance girly! Abby's writing is amazing! This story was so cute and wholesome - I read this in one sitting! Ali and Daniel had the best attraction, and every moment they shared was swoonworthy and magical. I loved that she was older - it made so much more believable. The fact that this book also tackled some deeper issues and in a way that was more advice than push. Every character had a story, and I'm excited to read the rest of the books in this series! I couldn't get enough of this and was so sad that it ended! Romance girls, you will not be disappointed. This book was so much more than just a romance!

Title: Breathe In, Bleed Out
Author: Brian  McAuley
My Review:
Horror/Slasher fans listen up!!! This was excellent! @brianmcwriter transcended this story to the next level, and it was bloody and gory and all the good things trauma is made of!! I love the inclusion of the retreat and all the puns relating to it! The characters were decidedly shady and flawed, which made the who dunnit a bit tricky to figure out. This was fast-paced, keep you on the edge of your seat action, and I loved it! I would highly recommend it was such an easy read chapters just flowed into each other - I love his writing style and will be back for more!

Thank you to @netgalley and @poisonedpenpress for this advanced copy in return for my honest opinion.

Title: The Dicktionary Club
Author: Sophie Gravia
My Review: This was hilarious! I loved the bestie vibe that @sophie.gravia_author created in this and that each of the friends brought their own unique complications to this book. Chick's before dick's was the central theme here, and it rounded off the premise of this book nicely. The chaos that these three girls created was hilarious and made for a very easy read! Philip was positively swoonworthy, and I can't lie, I wanted to strangle Ella a few times! All in all, this was a crazy yet funny, easy read with enough romance and magic to keep girls dreaming about their Philip in real life. Excellent choice for @bookscape.books July book of the month!

Title: Don't Trust Mother
Author: Carey Baldwin
My Review:
I love a good psychological thriller, and @authorcareybaldwin hit the mark with this one! I love that everything single character in this book was shady as hell it just added so much depth to the story. Nothing is what it seems here, and everyone is holding on to a secret of some kind. This was fast-paced, and the deception was deep and intriquid. @eliserothmusic ability to switch from sweet innocent Ivy to Seriously shady Sandra and then to raucous Richard is commendable, I loved her interpretation of each individual character. The ending was jaw-dropping, and I did not see all the twists coming. If you are looking for a seriously messed up family dynamic, then this one is for you... morally grey .... more like morally black!!

Thank you to @netgalley and @bookouture for this advanced copy in exchange for my honest opinion.

Title: The Nightingale
Author: Kristin Hannah
My Review: I love @kristinhannahauthor and her unapologetically real way that she writes her historical fiction books. This was a hard, long look into the past at what the war did and the battles that were fought by innocent people far away from the battlefield. Although this was not a true story, it was inspired by real events.  A hommage to what I'm sure must be countless stories untold of what brave but ordinary people did in these times. It puts a highlight on the stark reality of these times. I loved that this was not a romance but a story of survival and being brave and what that means.

Title: A Light in the Flame (Flesh and Fire, #2)
Author: Jennifer L. Armentrout
My Review: This series has my heart! I started my B&A and F&F journey at a very difficult time in my life, and honestly, these books have carried me through it. I really hope  @jennifer_l_armentrout realises the impact this series has made in my life, and I'm sure plenty others just like me, she has given us a heroine rising from the ash to be reborn as something majestic and powerful. It gives hope to us little guys that everything will be OK. Enough about the soppy stuff. Let's talk about the book!!  Can I just say Nyktos and Nektas can come and steal me from this realm any day! The Primal of Death with his swoonworthy Draken .... hell, yes, I want to go!! Sera goes through so much emotional turmoil, and Nyktos supporting her was epic! She unapologetically says and does what she wants (mostly!), and it emphasises her growth, which I believe is the point of this book. Nektas giving her very clever prompts makes me love him even more, and can I just just say him and Jadis together has my heart doing all the right things! I would highly recommend this series to anyone and everyone - You will not regret this journey but be warned .... it will destroy you in the best of ways!

Title: The Mother
Author: T.M. Logan
My Review: @tmloganauthor is an absolute master of  psychological thrillers, and after finishing this, I'm even more convinced! The characters are so likeable and flawed and most of the time morally grey. I can connect with them and envision myself in their shoes very easily. This story of a mother accused of murdering her husband and going to jail and trying to find out the truth after her release was incredible. The unravelling of this was fast-paced, and the fear for her slowly creeping in and settling was palpable. Slowly, as the pieces are being picked apart and put back together, you start to realise how deep the betrayle is, and it's so incredible, tragic, and sad. All I can say is incredible. If you are looking for a thriller that will have you spinning afterwards, then this is it!

Title: Leave No Trace (Kat and Lock, #2)
Author: Jo  Callaghan
My Review: Kat and Lock are back!! I love these two and how they compliment each other, Kate who is sometimes a bit over emotional and Lock bringing her straight back down to earth with his robot charm seeing things more black and white rather than grey. This time, they are let loose on a live case, and it is epic! Lock is learning more about human emotions, and it's sweet and endearing, like watching your baby learn to walk. Kat is learning how to reconnect with life, and Lock is a big help with this. The murders were gruesome, and the chase after the killer left me on the edge of my seat. @jocallaghankat has found the key to the future. AI is becoming an everyday reality, and her take on it with Aide Lock is cementing herself in the future!  I'm truly excited to read the next one.

Title: The Return of the King (The Lord of the Rings, #3)
Author: J.R.R. Tolkien

Title: The Undertaking of Hart and Mercy (Hart and Mercy, #1)
Author: Megan Bannen

Title: The Secret History
Author: Donna Tartt
My Review: The thing that annoyed me about this book is that the writing is amazing, and the story does not do it justice. We have a group of pretentious college students who I dislike immensely and the story of how they murdered their friend, who was probably the most interesting of them all. Apart from disliking the characters, I did not love the environment either - fancy English college with a select few applicants - this book screamed entitlement and bringing in the Greek theme just pushed it over that limit of "I am better, therefore I am" (Descartes) . All in all, this could have been said in 300 pages or less.

Title: The Two Towers (The Lord of the Rings, #2)
Author: J.R.R. Tolkien

Title: Liar Liar (Detective Harriet Blue, #3)
Author: James  Patterson
My Review: Harriet Blue is on a revenge mission, and it is epic! You have to read the whole series to understand where we are now. @jamespattersonbooks and @candicefoxauthor has crafted one of the best revenge series I've read in a while! I'm obsessed!  The Australian accent of the narrator makes this even better than it already is. I love the thrill of the violence, the plotting, and the payback. I am invested now and need to read the fourth and final book in the series. This is great for the vigilante readers out there - Harriet Blue is your next hero!

Title: Dirty Bad Wrong (Dirty Bad, #1)
Author: Jade West
My Review: Oh, my days, what nugget of deliciousness did I stumble across here! @jadewestauthor hits all the naughty kinks in more ways than one. I loved this!! Was I shocked ? Yes! Was I amazed - yes! Did I cringe ? Hell, yes! Was it spicy - oh hell, yes, all the spice this book was written in hell it's so spicy! Did I love it - a resounding double, yes! Will I go back for more? You can bet your ass I am - there are four books in the Dirty Bad series! Spice aside, this is such a cute romance. I loved every second of how this unfolds, and it hit all the feel-good spots. Ladies, proceed if you dare!! This is hectic, not gonna lie, the spice is next level, hardcore, BDSM! This book is seriously dirty, bad, wrong!!

Title: Ever After Glitched: Dark fairy tales and digital age horror stories
Author: Murphy Gray

Title: The Runaway Jury
Author: John Grisham
My Review: It took me a minute to finish this bad boy, but it was so rewarding. @johngrisham certainly knows how to keep your attention. This is written primarily in a third-person limited omniscient, which gave you just enough inside details of the current point of view character to make you speculate even more! This was a fascinating legal thriller ( possibly, my first one ever?!?!?) The amount of scheming that goes on in this novel is staggering, and it takes a brilliant mind to put it to pen. This had me guessing and on my toes from the start, and when the end came along, I was just as surprised as Fitch! Give it a go if you into non bloody thrillers. This will blow your mind!

Title: The Visitor (Jack Reacher #4)
Author: Lee Child
My Review: Reading a Reecher novel is equivalent to going to my happy place. The story is good. The characters are unique every time, and I never can guess where we will end up at the end of this. @jackreacherbooks has a winning formula, and I love it. Reacher is the most loveable , unlovable guy in the world, and yes , I love him. He is clever and down to earth, extremely helpful and compassionate. In this book, we see his softer side and how he was involved with female attacks on base. The murder of these women had me perplexed it did not add up, and in the end, I was shook. Excellent book, and as always, I can't wait to get the next one going.

Title: Part of Your World (Part of Your World, #1)
Author: Abby Jimenez

Title: When You Disappeared
Author: John Marrs
My Review:
This book shook my world. When I was done, I had to sit, take a minute, and process. @johnmarrs.author wrote the perfect psychological thriller. It should stick to you and invade your thoughts, and the horror should feel inflicted on yourself. I knew from the start something horrible had happened to force Simon to just up and walk away from his life, I commiserate with them both, and I felt the despair and the depression dripping from both. Heck, I even thought it's better this way - sometimes people just aren't compatible. I'm not saying it was done right im just saying there were valid reasons as far as I could tell. But as we go deeper, delve more, slowly the horrific truth starts trickling out. @simonmattacks narration, which started of as sounding uninterested and callous about leaving his wife, changed into a deliberate and detached psychopath. @elizabethknowelden narration is sweet and light yet passionate, and I could sense the trepidation when scenes permit. As far as psychological thrillers goes this was terrific! I can confirm that my pshyce has truly been touched and changed upon finishing this. Excellent reader for the fellow thrill seekers out there!

Thank you to @brilliancepublishing and @netgalley for this advanced copy in exchange for my honest opinion.

Title: And Then There Were None
Author: Agatha Christie
My Review: This is my first @officialagathachristie novel, and it was so wholesome! Being written in 1939, I expected some of it to be dated but was surprised at how well it has stayed relevant even now. The prose is precise and very posh, and I loved that too. A very enjoyable whodunit that stood the test of time. I will most certainly have a look at her other works.

Title: A Bargain So Bloody (Kingdom of Dark Magic, #1)
Author: Vasilisa Drake
My Review:
What the actual fuck just happened! How do you end a book on this cliffhanger!!! I am hooked, I want the next one, I want them all!!!! Very few times, I've had this feeling when finishing a book, and @vasilisadrakebooks gave me all the feels. We have slowburn romance, forced proximity, vampires, and witched and magical cards. The unrelenting tension and buildup were intoxicating, and the storyline was fantastic! @cecilysexily had the perfect fantasy drawl, and overall, it all just worked well. Fantasy girls, this is your time to shine. This book has it all - run to the shops - it was released on 30 June 2025!!

Thank you to @netgalley and @victoryeditingngc for my Advanced Copy in exchange for my honest opinion.

Title: The Ghostwriter
Author: Julie   Clark
My Review: This was brilliant!  Solving a 50 year old double murder and the prime suspect is your estranged father. I mean, how much more crazy can this get? I loved the dysfunctional family setup and how slowly unpacking the truth, not just about the murders but the reason for the family split. @julieclarkauthor has the slowburn psychological thriller down to a T! The story starts slowly, but at about 150 pages left, i could not stop, I just had to have one more chapter until it was done.  I did not anticipate the monumental twists at the end! Magnificent build of tension and mystery. This is a fantastic read for all the whodunit guys out there!

Title: The Very Best of Care
Author: Julie Hatch
My Review:
I had to take a moment after finishing this, just to organise my thoughts. This was an incredible debut by Julia Hatch. I was hooked from the first chapter and explosive start to a terrifying thriller. We go to the hospital and doctors for help trusting everything they say, and this book has just created a new fear. We are plunged into Sophie and Adam's world when their preemie Danny makes his arrival, and what transpires is a horrible unthinkable nightmare for any parent. This was heartstopping, I could not stop once i started, the setting, the patients, the parents, it felt like I was there with them beeping monitors in the back. Need a medical thriller to keep you up the whole night trying to finish it to know what happens next? This one has you covered!!

Thank you to @booksirens for my ARC in exchange for my honest opinion.

Title: The Dream Home
Author: T.M. Logan
My Review: We jump straight into a living nightmare with this one! Buying a house has never felt scarier after reading this. A secret room with a secret stash ... what would you do? @tmloganauthor sets such an intriguing scene that you can't help but follow in Adam's footsteps. Some of the things he did were just stupid (but that's men for you,  so I commiserate), but the rest... I mean, I am a curious gal, so I would probably have done the same. It just gets more mysterious and creepy with each page. There are so many twists and creepy people, so many potential suspects. I 100% was not expecting the culprit to be ... well, read it and see!  Brilliant read and brilliant author!

Title: Lula Dean's Little Library of Banned Books
Author: Kirsten Miller
My Review: What an absolute gem of a book! I think @kirstenmillerbooks did a fantastic job! She writes in such a way that you dont even realise the seeds of knowledge she is planting. This book deals with controversial issues in a light-hearted yet serious way. Subjects were approached in a way that broke down the stereotyping and explored why. I think this book is a national treasure and should be set reading in every high school out there. This should be gifted to the Karen's of our world, and maybe they can learn something. Lula will turn in her grave at the thought of this, and that makes it even better somehow. I dare you to read this and walk away unscathed. I have added some banned books to my tbr after this.

Title: Battling the Big Lie: How Fox, Facebook, and the MAGA Media Are Destroying America
Author: Dan Pfeiffer

Title: Nickel and Dimed: On (Not) Getting By in America
Author: Barbara Ehrenreich

Title: All That She Carried: The Journey of Ashley's Sack, a Black Family Keepsake
Author: Tiya Miles
