Title: Are You There God? It’s Me, Margaret
Author: Judy Blume
Publisher: Richard Jackson/Athaneum Books for Young Readers

Title: How the Word Is Passed: A Reckoning with the History of Slavery Across America
Author: Clint   Smith

Title: The Hemingses of Monticello: An American Family
Author: Annette Gordon-Reed

Title: Maus I: A Survivor's Tale: My Father Bleeds History (Maus, #1)
Author: Art Spiegelman

Title: The Diary of a Young Girl
Author: Anne Frank

Title: Clean Cut (Anna Travis, #3)
Author: Lynda La Plante
My Review: Voodoo and prostitutes, illegal immigrants, and police procedure failures, this mostly sums up this novel. We dive deeper into the Langton and Travis relationship, and I have to say im a bit disappointed in Langton and his actions in this book.  But in saying that, I have to applaud @lyndalaplantecbe for giving us real characters who are deeply flawed and sometimes morally grey. This did not take away from the story it added a different layer to the already complicated mess the two are faced with. This was still fast-paced and had me shocked at many instances. The fact that we even still today have issues with illegal immigrants made this book hit home in a sad way. It's really gripping read and would highly recommend it.

Title: The Frozen River
Author: Ariel Lawhon
My Review:
The thing I've found about reading a good historical fiction novel is that when you are done, it should feel like you have learned something, like you had a time machine back into the past and come back with an understanding of what happened. @ariel.lawhon did just that. Martha (our FMC)is a phenomenal storyteller in this book, and I got to experience life and the people from 1789. This is a story about the horrid and unjust things done back in those days and about a woman who tried to make a change. I highly recommend that you read this incredible story based on the life of Martha Ballard - midwife and healer 1735-1812.

Tiffany's Tales Book Club book of the month - June 2025

Title: All the Light We Cannot See
Author: Anthony Doerr

Title: The Alice Network
Author: Kate Quinn
My Review: I love a @katequinn5975 book. Her vivid descriptions of the people and the places transport me straight back in time, and it's a thrilling journey but also so sad. This book is based on the true story of the Alice Network in World War 1. Shocking to be faced with the stark cold truth of what women had to do and endure in the war to help their country. We  jump between 1915 and 1947, and the tale that unfolds is shocking and riveting. After reading about the real woman that this is based on, i have a newfound admiration for how the war was won. I love that I can learn facts about the past in the form of a fiction novel, and i can delve into history after being made aware of it.I urge you to do the same, these woman deserve to be known.

Title: Throttle Warriors: The Rise of the Storm Wolves
Author: Dazza Smith
My Review: I seldom review short stories, but I felt compelled to do so this time. @dazzatheunknownauthor is a very talented writer, and if I go by this short story , he has a promising career as an author ahead of him! This was faced paced and tense and had my adrenaline pumping. The pace of the book goes well with the racing theme. So much was said in so few words, and I guess my only issue was that it was not longer! The characters are likeable, and the story hit home for me. I'm excited to see what else he has written! Looking for a short break away from reality - this is it!

Title: Phantom (Cat and Mouse, #0)
Author: H.D. Carlton
My Review: This was beautiful, so I don't condone cheating at all... but if my husband was a low-life dick and he did that to me... I'm just saying.... I am a huge fan of Zade and Addie, and this felt like going home. I loved the mystery around this tale and how true love will always prevail. I'm a sucker for a bad boy, so that might also just be it. It's a feel-good happy romance (and yet we know different) that had me pining after my own phantom. 100% give it a read it made my day!

Title: 22 Seconds (Women’s Murder Club, #22)
Author: James  Patterson
My Review: I'm such a huge @jamespattersonbooks fan, and this series with @maxinepaetro is one of my favourites! This series follows the four women of the Woman's Murder Club, and in this novel, we are in for another faced paced,  crazy action, heartstopping ride. This focuses mainly on Lindsay and Joe. I loved getting to know the little family better!  There are as per usual side stories with the rest of the Club's girls, and I suspect the next book will be focused on Cindy's novel. Great read as always, loads of drama and action guaranteed!!

Title: The House in the Cerulean Sea (Cerulean Chronicles, #1)
Author: T.J. Klune
My Review: This was so cute! I loved every second of this! TJ Klune is amazing at World Building, and he creates place and people so vividly its like you are right there with them. All the children were unique, and I loved their different personalities and quirks  and definitely the sass they all have. It's a story about people who are different, and the moral lessons and little truths that are hidden all over this book was enrichment to my soul. The sarcasm was on point and had me chuckling a few times. I would suggest this to anyone who feels a little down or feels like they don't belong - this cute funny story will get you out of that lull.

Title: The Atlas Six (The Atlas #1)
Author: Olivie Blake
My Review: Holy shit! What did I just read. This book is so complex with so many layers and characters. It took me a while longer to finish this. The research @olivieblake have done to write this must have been crazy. We delf into philosophical and scientific questions about time and space and wormholes, about nature and nurture about ethical and unethical. The concepts in this book took me a minute to understand, and if you didn't, the next section would not make sense. The characters are so distinct and flawed and morally grey, and they all are very different from each other. The magic is incredible! If you understand what they do and how it will blow your mind. Absolute genius! Do yourself a favour and get this. It's complex, but it's worth it!

Title: Vera Wong's Unsolicited Advice for Murderers (Vera Wong, #1)
Author: Jesse Q. Sutanto
My Review:
My greatness, where do I even start! This book was incredible and funny and reminds me of my life and family. @jesseqsutanto
Took a simple story and crafted it into a tale. A tale of family - found and new - a tale of forgiveness and a tale of doing what's right. We all know a Vera. We all have a friend or family member who has to be involved in anything and everything and will pick at something until it's all the way unravelled. This was done in such a hysterically funny way you forget it's a tale about a death. You HAVE to listen to the audiobook @eunicewongnarration did such a fantastic job at the narration i was transposed to a different world where I could very vividly hear the voices and thanks to Jesse I could picture and smell this story with all my senses. This made me miss my Vera, and I thank you, ladies, for giving me this flashback. It's a brilliant book guys please go read it!

Title: The Four Winds
Author: Kristin Hannah
My Review: I can not put into words how much I love @kristinhannahauthor and her writing. Her books give you a look into the past with no bullshit.  The stark reality as harsh and hard as it is and it's written with so much poise you can't help getting emotional, you want to join the characters in their plight, you want to research this piece of history she has told you about and you cry for these people who you don't know or don't even exist because somehow history forgets them and wipes away the brutal reality of its victims so often. The great depression, the Dust Bowl era, the cotton picking 30s, call it what you want. As someone who has left her home and moved to another country for a better life - I get this book and I understand the emotions that comes with this and to then add on the challenges that nature and fellow humans gave these people. I'm not sure i would have made it if that was me, but then again, desperate times bring out the warrior in a person. Anyway, read the book and read any of her books. It's mind altering every, single, time!

Title: Claire Casey's Had Enough
Author: Liz Alterman
My Review:
This was brilliant! I had so much fun listening to this book! @lizalterman is a genius - here we have a day in the life of a midlife crisis 40 year old mom and everything that goes along with it, and let me tell you, she nailed it! Absolute perfection and also a sombering look at harsh reality. As a mom and as a woman, nobody ever tells you life and motherhood is hard, and it's a thankless job, and it's never ending. I think this made the book even funnier because I could envision myself in a couple of these scenarios. I celebrated all the wins with Claire as she is navigating through this day from hell. @stephrosevoice was the perfect narrator for this. I loved listening to her, and her subtle usage of tone and volume in the right place was incredible, I could feel the hysteria in her voice at some stage and the utter annoyance in others. If you are feeling burnt out, fed up, and tired and want to give in, listen to this ladies! I have a pep in my step i didn't yesterday, and this book is giving me a new lease on life! Brilliant writing, Liz. I can't thank you enough!

Thank you to @netgalley and @dreamscape_media for this ARC in exchange for my honest opinion.

Title: The Court that Bleeds Gold (The Gold Weaver, #1)
Author: Zara Storm
My Review:
What a refreshing change to dreary fantasy books I've read of late. Eleanor is like a breath of fresh air. I love the stories where a human crosses over into the faerie rhelm. Ruskin is everything a girl can dream about and not. This gave me Beaty and the beast vibes, and it's so good! I felt like a little girl again! @zarastormauthor knows how to capture and hold your attention from the first chapter. I loved the magic. I loved the curse that captured the land and figuring out how it came about. I loved Destan! What a hilarious character, and I loved how he would break a tense situation with his comments. @ashkingsleynarrator was magnificent as a narrator she lifted the book just that tad higher, and it all came together spectacularly.

Thank you to @netgalley and @victoryeditingngc for this ARC in exchange for my honest opinion.

Title: The Puppet Show (Washington Poe, #1)
Author: M.W. Craven
My Review: This is the start of something magnificent.  But I already knew that. I stumbled onto @m.w.craven books and read Dead Ground and immediately fell in love with Poe and Tilly. Now I know their origin, the start of what I'm sure is a once in a lifetime partnership. This book had me enthralled from the first chapter. Craven writes characters, landscapes, and crime scenes in such a vivid way I had no problem immersing myself into this story. The unravelling of the immolation man had me mesmerised. The layers in the story, the depravity of these men, the cunning of the killer. The twists and the jaw-dropping truths and relevations had me sitting at the end of this staring into space. I love this author, and I love this series and I want you to love it. Looking for a crime series with an admirable partnership, hilarious banter between them, unsuspecting twists, and truths, this is the one I promise you will not regret it!

Title: We Pretty Pieces of Flesh
Author: Colwill Brown
My Review: This book was incredibly frustrating. It's written in a South Yorkshire accent, and I could not for the life of me get on with it. Eventually, I switched to Audiobook, and even this was pretty bad as it's the author who does the narration.  It's so bad that it becomes the main focus instead of the actual story. It wasn't a  bad story to be fair, almost a biography of sorts. There were some funny stories and a few that I could envisage myself in, but not worth the effort it took for me to actually read this.

Title: No Way Back (Helen Grace, #5.5)
Author: M.J. Arlidge
My Review: Brilliant M.J. Arlidge does it again!! Fantastic short story giving you insight into the depths of  Helen Grace!

Title: The Night Shift
Author: Alex Finlay
My Review: This was an incredibly fast-paced, stacked full of suspense thriller. @alexfinlayauthor set the scene so beautifully, and it truly felt like a jump between years every time the point of view changed. There are so many perspectives, but it all comes together in a magnificent ending. If you are looking for edge of your seat kind of thriller look no more, this one has that covered.

Title: Death Row (Alibis Collection, #1)
Author: Freida McFadden
My Review: Brilliant Freida Mcfadden confusion in a very short dose! The queen of did-not-see-it-coming did it again.

Title: Fourth Wing (The Empyrean, #1)
Author: Rebecca Yarros
My Review: I've read this book so many times, and still, it makes me cry! Every. Damn. Time. I'm sure I can sit here and fawn over this book, but I'm not going to. Just go read the damn book! I did the graphic audio this time and just wow.... no words. Incredible!

Title: Little Boy Blue (Helen Grace, #5)
Author: M.J. Arlidge
My Review: Without a doubt, @m_j_arlidge has the power to enthral. This 5th book in the Helen Grace series did that and more. My favourite DI has to work the case of someone killed close to her heart. She is a complex woman with many layers, so that in itself says a lot. This is a faced paced edge of your seat novel, and I loved every second spiralling into the underbelly of BDSM. There are more than heart-stopping moments, and I'm delighted that Helen stays true to herself throughout. The cliffhanger at the end has me in knots, and I can't wait to start the next book!

Title: The Wall of Winnipeg and Me
Author: Mariana Zapata

Title: Dating Dr. Dil (If Shakespeare Was an Auntie, #1)
Author: Nisha Sharma

Title: The Seven Husbands of Evelyn Hugo
Author: Taylor Jenkins Reid
My Review: This book is one of those where you have to pause after finishing it. Where you can pay hommage to it. @tjenkinsreid wrote a book and created a character so profound that I still have to stop myself from looking her up on the Internet. Evelyn was written with so much passion, and her personalty was so vivid, she was real.... well, in my mind, at least. She was flawed, which to me was what almost made it real. She loved, she lived, got hurt, and hurt back. A life so full, well read the book, judge for yourself. I can't say more than this because it would give it away. It's a book about truth. Let me leave you with that to ponder on.

Title: Latency
Author: Chris Coppel
My Review: What piqued my interest when I read this synopsis was the crime thriller supernatural combo. @chris.coppel must have supernatural abilities because damn he delivered!! What a fascinating book this was! We have some gruesome, unexplained murders and two unlikely detectives thrown together and faced with the humongous task of solving the unsolvable. What transpires from their investigation is something surreal. I loved every second of this book! I want more, and I hope Chris is planning a second novel. I'd you want the unusual the supernatural combined with decent detectives doing their jobs with a tinge of humour this is the book for you!!

Title: Forest Ever White (Everlight, #3)
Author: Sara Knightly
My Review:
What I love about this series is how pure it is. @saraknightlybooks managed to create a modern-day fairy tale for all audiences and its perfection! It's a story about friendship, growing and learning about yourself, and yes, a little love as well! A true adventure and the world building is phenomenal - I can very vividly picture the forest, and I had real angst about the count. And then the magic! The magic is unique, and as far as I'm concerned, pure. The narration by @kimchurchillvoice was spectacular and created a real immersive experience. This series is a must-read for anyone needing to believe again. Believe in honour, believe in friendship, and believe in love.

Thank you to @netgalley and Portal Publishing for this ARC in exchange for my honest opinion.

Title: Where's Molly (Cat and Mouse, #2.5)
Author: H.D. Carlton
My Review: I love H.D. Carlton and I love this series! This book was very vanilla compared to Haunting Adeline & Hunting Adeline, i have to say, but dont get me wrong, there was still enough depravity and sex to make it a true H.D.Carlton book. But what I love even more is the fact that there is such an amazing heartfelt story behind the sex and blood and the gore. Here, we got the story of Molly and what happened to her and how she fits into the whole cat and mouse series. And yes, I will be reading the other two as well! I can not recommend this enough, but be warned it is a very dark romance - you have been warned!

Title: The Pryce of Conceit  (The Pryce of Murder, #1)
Author: Kari Bovee

Title: Tomorrow, and Tomorrow, and Tomorrow
Author: Gabrielle Zevin
My Review: I struggled with this one. I'm not a big romance reader, but I can't even blame that. I just didn't get it. It spans over a large amount of time, and I think it was just too much info for me to cope with. It's also set in the gaming world, and I have played some games in my life, but nothing that compares to what they are talking about in this book. I got lost trying to figure out what they were talking about. I think this book will be very good for the younger generation - it contains a lot of phrases that my son uses. It's a really sweet yer tragic love story. Don't get me wrong, I just didn't get the fluff that accompanied it.

Title: The Cure: The chilling, powerful new speculative thriller from the author of ONE
Author: Eve   Smith
My Review:
What I love about every @evesmithauthor book is the chilling reality that this could possibly happen in the future. The fine line between the dystopian speculative fiction and a terrifying reality is something she is a master at creating. In this book, we get faced with a cure for ageing. As good as this sounds, Eve sets the scene to show what this would realistically entail, and it is scary! The palpable sense of dread that starts at the beginning of this book and keeps growing till the very last word is intoxicating, and I still can't stop thinking about it. Her books leave you with an array of existential questions to ponder, which, in my eyes, is the highest form of praise to any author. I would highly recommend this to just about everyone - this is one of the best books I've read this year.

Tiffany's Tales Book Club book of the month - May 2025

Title: The Horse and His Boy (Chronicles of Narnia, #5)
Author: C.S. Lewis

Title: Save Her Life (Sandra Vos, #1)
Author: Carolyn Arnold
My Review:
Oh my greatness! What an incredible action-packed book! @authorcarolynarnold had me on the edge of my seat straight from the first chapter. The different scenarios that we get included in all add up to the exhilarating epic conclusion of the debut novel. Sandra Vos is my new favourite FBI agent, a hostage negotiater, which is such a welcome change to just your normal familiar agent. She is a calm yet terrifying person with an incredible mind. I loved that Arnold set each scene for us in such detail, and we get to experience the inside of these hostage situations from the law's side. I have to give credit to @kathrynakin01 for her phenomenal narration skills! She knew exactly what to do and how to say it, which added so much value to the book. I felt the tension, I could hear the suspense and fear! The only negative thing I can say is why have I only now discovered Carolyn Arnold!! She has an impressive list of novels behind her name, and I am so excited to see what else she has for me! Guys, if you are looking for an action-packed, suspenseful female FBI series.... this is the one! I suggest you run to the shops right now, go on, go!!!

Thank you to @netgalley and @bookouture for this ARC in exchange for my honest opinion.

Title: The Thursday Murder Club (Thursday Murder Club, #1)
Author: Richard Osman
My Review: Unpopular opinion, but I didn't like it. I really struggled. Don't get me wrong - the story had good points, and there were twists that I did not expect. But it still was not enough. There are a lot of people - too many, in my opinion, it over complicated it for me. I didn't like the narrator. I really wanted to like it but sometimes that is not enough.

Title: Under the Whispering Door
Author: T.J. Klune
My Review: This book has turned my world upside down. We've all lost someone and went through grief in different ways, but did you ever stop and think about what the person who died was going through. This is a book about death, loss, grief, and yes, even love. TJ Klune took a sensitive subject and made it manageable. Who knows what really happens in the after? What I can tell you is that this book made me see things in a different light. It was sad but wholesome, and I would advise you to read it with an open mind. It's a feel-good story despite the sensitive subject.

Title: Bodies
Author: Jed Mercurio
My Review: I did not expect this when I picked up this book, and yet it's almost fitting that you don't know what to expect. Jed Mercurio takes us through the life of a junior doctor. The pressure from the job, the exhaustion after every shift, the fear of mistakes made, and the sex - raw and messy and anatomically correct. This is not a soft and happy book. This is raw, scary, dark, and frank, even funny if you get the humour. I loved it because of all of this. A glimpse into a life told in so much detail and accuracy it's scary. Read it if you dare. This will most certainly live in my head rent-free for some time.

Title: Bad Men (Bad Men, #1)
Author: Julie Mae Cohen
My Review: I love Julie Mae Cohen books! She is a wizard at making the obvious seem unimportant but yet still very important. Let me explain, we have Saffy, who is a serial killer by night, but the book is about her being lonely and finding the right guy for herself. I also thought that she might want to kill him at first, but no, she wanted love! I had empathy for Saffy, and I wanted her to have all the good in life. Yes, this is another serial killer FMC, but it was so much more. If you are a romantic at heart but can handle the trauma, then this is for you! Serial killers deserve to be happy, too!

Title: The Curfew
Author: T.M. Logan
My Review: I could not put this down! T.M.Logan had me on the edge of my seat all the way through this book. As a parent, I know what fear I have every day he leaves the house. Maybe because this book was a subject that was close to home, and on top of that, it was a very real-life scenario that played out. The anguish from the parents and the fear from the children were palpable. If you are looking for a very good psychological thriller, look no more. I am definitely getting his other books as soon as I can.

Title: ALL QUITE ON THE WESTERN FRONT
Author: Erich Maria Remarque
My Review: War is devastating and senseless. This account from a German soldier is heartbreaking. The reality of what it means to go to war. The loss not just of life but of innocence. It's a powerful book, and I hope we never have to know what this feels like.

Title: 21st Birthday (Women's Murder Club, #21)
Author: James  Patterson
My Review: Happy 21st book! This series is one of my absolute favourites. The four women of the Murder club feel like they are part of my family now. Each of them always bring their unique talents to the investigation. This case involves a mother and little girl, and it is heartbreaking. With the group of four out to solve it , you can be guaranteed to an interesting story!

Title: Protection Song
Author: Bill  Randall
My Review:
What an incredible start to a writing career for Bill Randall! I was invested from the start, and I felt Ian's pain and anguish as he went through the book. I felt it was incredible that he had this pull towards Ellie and her story. Marlon was such a good edition, also showcasing who Ellie was truly. I loved the music aspect and how involved the storytelling was about setup and gigs. The mystery kept coming, adding in new characters as the story developed. I did not figure it out until the end. A massive bonus is the song at the end. It just rounded it off nicely, and it felt like I personally knew the people.
Thank you to Booksirens for my ARC it was amazing, guys!

Title: Everyone in the Group Chat Dies
Author: L.M. Chilton
Publisher: Head of Zeus -- an Aries Book
My Review: I struggled with this. The premise is good, very similar to - I know what you did last summer. But it just didn't hold my attention. It's written in two timelines, then and now, but I struggled to keep up with which is which. Its a twist mystery with a few good reveals and a big twist in the end. I was just not a fan.

Title: In the Blink of An Eye (Kat and Lock, #1)
Author: Jo  Callaghan
My Review: I did not know what to expect from this one. Ai and detective combo? But... it was phenomenal!! Jo Callaghan captivated me from the first word till the last! What an intriguing concept - bringing AI detectives onto real-life cases - something that could be in our future if the rate of AI development continues. What really got me is that she showcased the good and the bad of both roles. DCS Kat and Lock starts off as a highly volatile combo and, in an almost sweet and caring way, turn into the robot/cop dream team. The story was gripping and the pace fast. I did this book in a day, and I want more!!! This was a refreshing change to the usual police procedural crime thrillers but not so much as to make it unrealistic. Well done, what an excellent debut.

Title: The Red Dahlia (Anna Travis, #2)
Author: La Plante. Lynda.
My Review: I can honestly say I have found one of my new favourite crime series! Anna Travis is a girl to my heart. @lyndalaplantecbe is so meticulous in the description of the procedures and the characters that you can't help but be pulled in by the story. I found the connection to the Black Dahlia case fascinating - I've read a few series killer non-fiction books, and to date, it is still unsolved. Then our boy Langton makes a come back, and I am thrilled!!! The banter between the two of them makes this book even better. This series is starting to feel like home, and I want to jump right back in with the next one. If you are looking for a series to obsess over, then step up and come get it. This one is for you.

Title: Cursed Fates (Zodiac Academy, #5)
Author: Caroline Peckham
My Review:
How much emotional damage can one book give you ... and I'm not even considering the whole series because that is a whole different conversation. This people right here is why adults are allowed to drink!
@carolinepeckham and @susanne_valenti are diabolical. But yet I come back for the next book every time, and I mourn the losses, and I heal, and I process the heartach along with each of the characters. Cursed Fates is such an apt title for this bloody book!! What did Darcy do to you, dear authors, the poor lass, I'm shattered along with her. And then Tory .... that ending, I mean ... no words ... what the actual fuck.... cliffhanger my ass more like clifftastrophy. An honourable mention has to go to the heirs they have really stepped up and especially Caleb ... I mean, that is what friends are for .... Seriously though, if you haven't started Zodiac Academy, you should. Like now .... really!!!

Title: Little Women (Little Women, #1)
Author: Louisa May Alcott
Additional Authors: Marta Fihel, Grzegorz Komerski, Regina Barreca

Title: The House of My Mother: A Daughter's Quest for Freedom
Author: Shari Franke
My Review: In this biography, Shari Franke tells us the story of her mother, Ruby Franke, and what horrendous things she did to her kids. Ruby had a YouTube channel where she documented her families day to day life. She exploited and abused these children for her own gain and was finally arrested and convicted. This was a very emotional read, and I was shocked and appalled by what this poor child went through. I'm glad those kids are now out of that situation, and hopefully, they can mend and have long, prosperous lives.

Title: Fifty Fifty (Detective Harriet Blue, #2)
Author: James  Patterson
My Review: I love the fast paced chapters which right from the first word puts you into a gruelling fast paced read. @jamespattersonbooks and @candicefoxauthor make an incredible duo and I love the Australian setting of Detective Harriet Blue's books. Having left us with a cliffhanger in Never Never I had to jump right back in and boy it just keeps you on your toes the whole time! Duel timeline so you follow what is happening with Sam and what Harriet is doing after being banished to the outback again. She needs to find a bomber in a small town of 75 and the unravelling of this is heartbreaking! The two timelines gloriusly come to a heartstopping conclusion in the last few chapters and it's shocking and devastating and yes ... you guesed it .. another cliffhanger.  I love Harriet's character so much, I will definitely be reading number 3.

Title: The Women
Author: Kristin Hannah
My Review:
When you start a Kristin Hannah
book, you are guaranteed a book filled to the brim with raw, heartbreaking subject matter. A story told in such a way that you envision every element down to even the smells. And this tear jerker was no different. I felt the anguish and the fear, I revelled in the triumph, and I fell in love along with Frankie every time. Every deceit, every lie, and loss I felt with her. My father was in the military, and I know what my mother went through when he came back and what he did to get away from that horror. To be a woman back in those days, having gone through these things because you want to help and then being told you did not matter. I applaud every single woman who did this, and I'm saying you matter, we see you. This book is not a romance it's a book that tells a story of a woman who survived and how she did it, and it was glorious!! Thank you for this incredible book.

Title: The Assassin's Shadow (Legends of the Shadow, #1)
Author: R.S. Moule
My Review:
What an epic start to a new Fantasy series!

I was given this ARC by @netgalley and @bookouture.

The story reminded me of playing Diablo with my son, with orcs and vampires and magical Elvin. The detail going into these creatures was amazing, and the world building phenomenal. @rs_moule had my full attention from the start.

It starts with Cetrik, who is seeking the deadliest man alive, Locan A'Shadow, and what transpires when he finds him. There are battles from the word go, politics that will blow your mind, and of course, some family drama! I was tense the whole time, just waiting for the next thing to happen.

The narrator did such a good job with the different voices of the characters. It really felt like I was right there with the little band of misfits as they trudged through the land on their quest.

If you are looking for high fantasy with epic battles, intriguing political viewpoints, then this is for you! I can't wait to see what else these two will get up to.
